body.title: CLOSED: NE grader pass in coa.py
body.result: ne_grader scores 3 dimensions via haiku, logs to psych_log, escalates on low scores
body.description:
After `_ne_worker` produces a non-empty NE result, spawn a `_grader_worker` in a fresh NE call that evaluates output against a hardcoded rubric: did it identify a concrete next action? did it surface the current stuck point? does the recommended habit exist in the registry? If grader returns weak output, call `escalate_to_channel()` with the gap description.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py`
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; rubric hardcoded initially; grader runs in a separate NE invocation (not same `_ne_thread`); must check `_ne_thread.is_alive()` before spawning grader; grader swallows its own exceptions (must never crash caller)
**Scope boundary:** IN — grader function in coa.py, escalate on weak NE output. OUT — no new DB tables, no rubric persistence, no changes to NE prompt itself.
**Test plan:** unit test grader fires and escalates on weak (empty next_action) NE output fixture; unit test grader passes silently on strong output fixture
